title: Lambda Functions Practice
instructions: Practice using lambda functions with map, filter, and sorted.
hints: map() applies a function to every item
filter() keeps items where function returns True
sorted() accepts a key parameter for custom sorting

testCases.description: square_all should return squared values using map and lambda
testCases.validation: assert square_all([1, 2, 3, 4]) == [1, 4, 9, 16], 'Should square all numbers in the list'

testCases.description: filter_evens should return only even numbers using filter and lambda
testCases.validation: assert filter_evens([1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]) == [2, 4, 6], 'Should filter to only even numbers'

testCases.description: sort_by_length should sort words by their length
testCases.validation: assert sort_by_length(['banana', 'pie', 'apple', 'go']) == ['go', 'pie', 'apple', 'banana'], 'Should sort words shortest to longest'

testCases.description: sort_by_age should sort user dicts by age field
testCases.validation:
users = [{'name': 'Bob', 'age': 30}, {'name': 'Alice', 'age': 20}]
assert sort_by_age(users) == [{'name': 'Alice', 'age': 20}, {'name': 'Bob', 'age': 30}], 'Should sort users by age ascending'

testCases.description: square_all should return empty list for empty input
testCases.validation: assert square_all([]) == [], 'Should return empty list for empty input'

testCases.description: filter_evens should return empty list when all numbers are odd
testCases.validation: assert filter_evens([1, 3, 5, 7]) == [], 'Should return empty list when no evens exist'

description: Practice using Python lambda functions with built-in higher-order functions like map(), filter(), and sorted(). Implement concise one-line functions to square numbers, filter even values, sort words by length, and sort dictionaries by key — essential functional programming patterns in Python.
